title: Is Balsamic-dressing Gluten Free? | BiteRight
description: Public gluten safety analysis for Balsamic-dressing. See major risks, safer alternatives, and what to ask before ordering.
heading: Is Balsamic-dressing gluten free?
intro: This public analysis report explains the biggest gluten risks in Balsamic-dressing and how to order more safely.

verdict.summary: Balsamic dressing can be safe, but there is a risk of gluten contamination from certain ingredients or cross-contact during preparation. Always check labels and inquire about preparation methods.

disclaimer: This guidance is informational only. Always verify ingredients and preparation with the restaurant.

sections.title: Quick answer
sections.body: Balsamic-dressing can vary by recipe, ingredients, and cross-contact controls in the kitchen.

sections.title: Common gluten risks
sections.body: Watch for hidden sources like soy sauce, malt flavoring, marinades, thickeners, and shared fryers.

sections.title: How BiteRight helps
sections.body: Scan menus and ingredient labels with BiteRight to get a localized gluten-risk breakdown in seconds.

risk: Balsamic vinegar (if made with gluten-containing grains)
Thickeners or stabilizers that may contain gluten

waiter_script.preview: Can you confirm if the balsamic dressing is gluten-free and prepared in a dedicated area?

faq.question: Can BiteRight confirm if Balsamic-dressing is gluten free?
faq.answer: BiteRight highlights likely gluten risks based on ingredients and preparation. Always confirm with the kitchen if you have coeliac disease.

faq.question: What should I ask a restaurant?
faq.answer: Ask about shared equipment, sauces, marinades, and whether the kitchen has a dedicated gluten-free prep area.
